semanticCore: Slowness, respite, molten metal, dregs of oil, or ease
quranicRelevance: Granting respite/delay to disbelievers (Mahhala/Amhila - 'famah-hili al-kafireena amhilhum'), and drink like molten brass/metal (Muhl - 'khal-muhli').

keyDerivations.meaning: To grant respite, delay, give time, or defer punishment
To respite, give a brief delay, or let be
Molten metal, boiling brass, or dregs of hot oil

figurativeOrRareUsages: فَمَهِّلِ الْكَافِرِينَ أَمْهِلْهُمْ رُوَيْدًا (86:17): So grant respite/delay to the disbelievers; respite them gently/briefly (divine strategy).
يَشْوِي الْوُجُوهَ بِئْسَ الشَّرَابُ (18:29): It scalds/roasts the faces, miserable is the drink (Muhl).
discardedNoise: Obscure details of camel fat extraction.
summaryEn: The root represents slowness and suspension, covering the strategic respite (Israa/Ihmal) granted to tyrants, and the boiling, molten metal drink (Muhl) of Hell.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر التمهل والإبطاء والمذاب، واصفاً الإمهال الإلهي المؤقت للكافرين (أمهلهم)، والشراب المغلي كالمعادن المذابة في جهنم (كالمهل).
rawText: أَمْهَلَهُ 4 He acted gently, softly, or in a leisurely manner, towards, or with, him. (K.) He granted him some delay, or respite; let him alone, or left him, for a while. (S, K.) [In both senses] i. q. أَرْوَدَهُ. (S, art. رود.) You say أَمْهِلْنِى حَتَّى أَفْعَل كَذَا Grant thou me some delay that I may do such a thing; give me time to do such a thing. See Har p. 164. تَمَهَّلَ 5 He acted, or behaved, deliberately, or leisurely; without haste; (S, Msb, K;) in an affair. (S, Msb.) غُرْمُولٌ مُتْمَهِلٌّ A hard penis: see قَاسِبٌ. مَهَلٌ : see مَهْلٌ. مَهْلٌ and 🡻مَهَلٌ and 🡻مُهْلَةٌ Gentleness; a leisurely manner of acting or proceeding. (Msb, K, &c.) === مَهْلًَا Act gently, softly, or leisurely. مُهْلَةٌ : see مَهْلٌ. === فِى الأَمْرِ مُهْلَةٌ In the affair is a delay; syn. تَأْخِيرٌ. (Msb.) === فِى مُهْلَةٍ Leisurely; gently; by little and little.
